msgs.text:
I treated the prior turns as a proposal log, not as a proof. The promptвҖҷs submission constraints are fixed: sigma,tau are the distinguished tame generators, the tame relation tau^sigma = tau^2 is implicit, later generators must lie in the pro-2 core, and constants are reduced modulo M=85667662080.The prior log leaves only one serious unrefuted candidate, the вҖңdynamic tame-inertiaвҖқ word from Turns 9вҖ“46; earlier scalar, plain-ПҖ2, and fixed-П„вҲ’2 variants were refuted by explicit finite-tame or index-two tests.

The live candidate is still variables sigma,tau,x,y definitions nx = (x*tau^50932630783)^40491355905 ny = (y*tau)^40491355905 relations x*sigma^45176306175*x*sigma^40491355905*x^-1*nx*ny^sigma*y^-1

Equivalently, with ПҖ=ПҖ2вҲҲZ and О·=2ПҖ on odd tame inertia,r=xПғвҲ’ПҖxПғПҖxвҲ’1(xП„вҲ’О·)ПҖ ((yП„)ПҖ)ПғyвҲ’1,where ПҖвүЎ40491355905,вҲ’ПҖвүЎ45176306175,вҲ’О·вүЎ50932630783(modM).The 2-primary coordinate of О· is immaterial in this word, because П„ has prime-to-2 finite tame images.

A stable correction: the two quotient formulas The recent turns repeatedly oscillate on two fractions. I fixed the convention as follows.For the sign-cover computation, let V=kerОә, let t be the deck involution, and restrict to a nontrivial odd tame-inertia character block П„вҶҰОұоҖ =1. ForNa (u)=(uП„a)ПҖ,the Fox coefficient isDa=j=0вҲ‘ПҖвҲ’1(tОұa)j.

The geometric-series identity is (1вҲ’tОұa)Da=1вҲ’(tОұa)ПҖ.

Since ПҖвүЎ1(mod2) and ПҖвүЎ0(mode), one has (tОұa)ПҖ=t. ThereforeDa=(1вҲ’t)(1вҲ’tОұa)вҲ’1.This is the direction that makes the unit/radical argument legitimate: 1вҲ’tОұa is a 2-adic unit for ОұaоҖ =1, while 1вҲ’t is radical. Hence Da is radical, and the coefficient fromxвҲ’1(xП„вҲ’О·)ПҖ is вҲ’1+DвҲ’О·, a unit. Thus all nontrivial odd tame-character blocks contribute only unit Smith pivots.For the trivial tame-inertia block, with T the Пғ-cycle operator and sвүЎПҖ(modf), the mod-2 linear relation is(1+TвҲ’s)X+(1+T)Y=0.

If f=2a m with m odd, then sвүЎ1(mod2a) and sвүЎ0(modm). For even f, s is odd, so 1+TвҲЈ1+TвҲ’s. The valid elimination is thereforeY=Ps (T)X+YЛү,Ps(T)=1+T1+TвҲ’s,up to the harmless convention TвҶ”TвҲ’1. The reciprocal is not valid in F2 [T]/(TfвҲ’1).With h(T)=1+Ps (T)+Ps(TвҲ’1),for any nontrivial f-th root О¶,(1+О¶)h(О¶)=О¶вҲ’s+О¶s+1=О¶вҲ’s(1+О¶2s+1).But 2s+1 is odd and congruent to 1(modm), so gcd(f,2s+1)=1. Hence h has no nontrivial f-th-root zero and h(1)=1. The trivial block is nonsingular with this convention.These corrected formulas keep the prior abelian consequences intact:Ue,fabвүғZ2ef+1вҠ•C2,and for the sign kernel,(kerОә)abвүғZ22ef+1вҠ•C4.New contribution: closed formula for the first marked IA ambiguity

Turn 46 computed several examples of the first residual marked IA ambiguity after odd-Hall rigidity. I found a clean general description.LetО”e,f=вҹЁПғЛү,П„ЛүвҲЈП„Лүe=1,ПғЛүf=1,ПғЛүП„ЛүПғЛүвҲ’1=П„Лү2вҹ©,with e odd and eвҲЈ2fвҲ’1. Write f=2a m, m odd, and let

H=вҹЁП„Лү,ПғЛү2aвҹ©be the odd Hall subgroup, so P=О”/HвүғC2a. After odd-Hall rigidity, the first residual marked IA ambiguity isAe,f=H1 (P,HomH(F2 [О”]вҠ•F2b,L2)),whereL1вүғF2 [О”]вҠ•F2 bвҠ•F2S,S=Пғf is marked, andL2=(L1[2]вҠ•вҲ§2L1)/вҹЁqвҹ©,q=gвҲҲО”вҲ‘Xg[2]+[b,S].

As Turn 46 observed, the regular part contributes no H1, soAe,fвүғH1(P,L2H).

The nonfree part of L2H is especially simple. Besides the two universal trivial classes b[2]and S[2], every further nonfree P-summand comes from an involution in О”e,f.

Indeed, H-orbits on unordered pairs in О” give the (вҲ§2F2[О”])H part. A P-orbit of such pair-orbits is nonfree exactly when the relative displacement dвҲҲО” satisfies d=dвҲ’1, i.e. when d is an involution. Non-involutive displacements produce free P-orbits and hence no H1.

The involutions in О”e,f occur only when f is even. They are the elements П„iПғf/2 satisfying(П„iПғf/2)2=П„ i(1+2f/2)=1.

Thus their number is

I(e,f)=gcd(e,2f/2+1).

Consequently, for f even,dimF2Ae,f=2+gcd(e,2f/2+1).For f odd, the residual even quotient P is trivial after odd-Hall rigidity, so there is no such P-operator ambiguity.

This formula recovers the prior examples:(e,f)(1,2)(3,2)(3,4)(3,6)(5,4)(15,4)gcd(e,2f/2+1)131355dimAe,f353577 This is a useful sharpening of the proof gap. The large operator matrix in mixed cases is not mysterious at first IA level: the only nonfree directions are the two universal square directions plus the directions indexed by involutions in the finite tame quotient.

For (e,f)=(3,2), these involutions are П„i Пғ, producing the three Zd directions from the prior turns. For (e,f)=(5,4), the involutions are П„iПғ2, iвҲҲZ/5, producing five mixed directions plus the two universal ones, hence dimension 7.This does not prove the candidate, but it makes the remaining formation problem more explicit: the exact arithmetic filtered P-action must match the candidateвҖҷs choices in these involution-indexed directions, and then in their higher restricted-power descendants.

ESTABLISHED: The live dynamic candidate remains unrefuted. The stable formulas areDa=(1вҲ’t)(1вҲ’tОұa)вҲ’1,Ps(T)=1+T1+TвҲ’s (up to TвҶ”TвҲ’1). With these, the prior conclusionsUe,fabвүғZ2ef+1вҠ•C2,(kerОә)abвүғZ22ef+1вҠ•C4 remain consistent. New this turn: the first marked IA ambiguity has the closed dimension formuladimF2Ae,f=2+gcd(e,2f/2+1)for f even, with the non-universal directions indexed by involutions of О”e,f.

GAP: This is still not a proof of the presentation of GQ2. The missing ingredient remains a dyadic formation/rigidity theorem, or an explicit arithmetic comparison, proving that the candidateвҖҷs exact filtered tame action realizes the actual arithmetic Frobenius/tame-inertia action for all finite tame subextensions.

NEXT: Use the involution-indexed description to compute the candidateвҖҷs actual first IA class uniformly: two universal square coordinates plus one coordinate for each involution П„iПғf/2. Then compare those coordinates with explicit Kummer/Witt arithmetic, starting with (e,f)=(5,4), where the five involution directions are the first substantially larger mixed test.[BREAKTHROUGH]
